raw_consumer_statement: I received a Jet Blue credit card in the mail from Barclays bank that I did not apply for XX/XX/year>. I called the bank 's phone number on the paperwork and spoke with a male representative with an XXXX  about my concerns. I told him that I did not apply for a credit card but it looked like this is what was sent in the mail. He asked me " did you recently go on a cruise ''. I thought this was a strange question, but I had gone on a cruise with my husband in XXXX so I said " yes. '' He told me " oh well this was just a promotion and not a credit card ''. I wanted clarification stating " So this is not a credit card its just a promotional thing that was sent? ". Again he stated " yes, there is no balance on the card, it is closed and you can shred it. '' I was relieved and then I did shred the card but I did take a picture of it first because I thought it was odd. Several weeks later I saw that my credit score was dropping and I received several credit card denial letters-cards I never applied to and realized that someone was using my information to apply for cards. Luckily the other cards were denied but the Barclays Jet Blue card that I was told was not a card now showed to have a balance of {$4800.00} on it. I put a freeze on my credit with XXXX. I called Barclays and reported the card and charges were fraudulent with XXXX. XXXX told me that the card was not closed initially because of fraud but had been closed " because you told us you had to many credit cards ''. This was a complete false statement and I never closed the card because initially I was told it was never opened and that it was a " promotion ''. After waiting for Barclays fraud investigation they sent a letter XX/XX/year> stating that their investigation showed that the card and charges were " not fraudulent ''. They continue to report late payments to credit bureaus and worsen my credit score. When I googled " Jetblue Mastercard Scam '' I learned of several other people that were also scammed this same way in Spring-Summer 2024. I have also received letters from 3 different companies that my personal information was hacked in the past 10 months and identity is at risk. I have attached paperwork from various cards that were applied for fraudulently as well as the notices that my personal information was at risk.
